summary: Early documented ancestor in the Amir Nezam Letgahi chain.
Son of Haji Mohammad Khan Jeyhunabadi in the published lineage.
Known by the title E'temad al-Saltaneh; father of Abdollah Khan.
Founder/leading figure of the Amir Nezam Letgahi family; associated with Letgah and Kabudarahang.
Son of Abdollah Khan; titled Ejlal al-Mamalek and Amir Arfa'.
Son of Abdollah Khan; titled Sardar Akram. No descendants are shown in the book's figure.
Brother of Gholamhossein Khan according to user-verified family tree.
Paternal grandfather of the user; connected under Mohtaj Ali Khan by user correction.
Father of Hossein Gholi Amiri Gharagozloo; born in Hamadan.
Project owner and family historian.
Named in the user-supplied ancestral chain above Haji Mohammad Khan; exact published link remains to be tested.
Son of Abdollah Khan; Ejlal al-Dowleh and Amir Tuman; later titled Sa'ed al-Saltaneh.
Son of Mohammad Hossein Khan; senior Gharagozloo commander killed after being severely wounded in the Herat campaign.
Son of Nabi Khan and descendant of Mohammad Hossein Khan; later a prominent Qajar official.
Yavar who supported Nabi Khan’s forces at Herat, was wounded, and received the rank of sarhang. Identity is not yet merged with other Abdollah Khans.
Gharagozloo soldier who repeatedly climbed the Herat fortress wall and attempted to plant the victory flag.
Colonel of the Mokhberan regiment wounded in the leg during the Herat fighting.
Gharagozloo brigadier active in the southern campaigns against British forces.
Son of Rustam Khan; senior Gharagozloo officer active in southern Iran and Kerman.
Commander of the Dargazin infantry regiment in the Laristan campaign.
Commander associated with the sixth Gharagozloo regiment in Kerman.

preferred_name_en: Mohammad Hossein Khan Hosam al-Molk Gharagozloo
summary: Son of Ali Khan; Gharagozloo officer rewarded for service in Kerman.

preferred_name_en: Zeyn al-Abedin Khan Hosam al-Molk Gharagozloo
summary: Prominent Gharagozloo khan active in Kermanshah and Hamadan politics.
Son of Zeyn al-Abedin Khan; married Qamar al-Saltaneh in 1309 AH.
Gharagozloo officer with the rank of yavar, involved in a diplomatic incident with the Russian embassy in 1313 AH.
Servant of Aqa Jan Gharagozloo involved in the death of a Russian coachman.
Russian coachman killed during an altercation involving Hasan Tupchi.
